title: alnus hirsuta
intro: Alnus hirsuta, also known as the hairy alder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas. The medicinal properties of Alnus hirsuta lie in its bark, leaves, and roots, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Alnus hirsuta can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional folk medicine, to treat conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
